     pane geometry, what was on screen), plain + SGR variants, expected classification.
     4. Write the issue comment: attach/inline the frames, state the correction with
     the msg-948 probe + the version-drift find, and point at the TOCTOU window in
     inbox_service.py. Frames + correction only — not pushing our FX3 fix design
     (per user decision this session).
     5. User posts it (or approves me posting) — public, outward-facing.

     Files

     - Read-only: blueprints/fx3-inbox-delivery-race.md,
     cli-agent-orchestrator/test/fixtures/fx3/,
     cli-agent-orchestrator/src/cli_agent_orchestrator/providers/claude_code.py
     - New: tmp/orch/frames/ corpus + tmp/orch/issue-405-reply.md

     Verification

     - Every claimed frame is grep-checked for ↑/↓ to navigate before the claim ships —
     no assertion about a frame that isn't in the corpus.
     - The version-drift claim is checked against both captures (2.1.205 fixture vs 2.1.209
     fresh), not asserted from memory.
     - Negative frames are run against the actual current pattern to confirm they don't
     match today.

● Plan approved — and that ExitPlanMode dialog is the frame we needed. Let me see what the capture caught.

  Ran 1 shell command

● Major find — and it flips part of my own analysis. Let me verify rigorously before claiming anything.

  Ran 1 shell command

● My test is unreliable — -qc is contradictory and the pane scrollback may still hold the earlier dialog. Let me check where that footer actually appears.

  Searched for 1 pattern

● Confirmed — and this is better evidence than I expected. Let me capture one more frame: right now my pane shows the plan text with no dialog open.
